Classiq Quantum Office Hours
Classiq Quantum Office Hours is a recurring, product-focused webinar series that shows how real quantum applications are built using Classiq, from idea to execution.  

[Register now](https://get.classiq.io/office-hour-webinars/#lp-code-160)
[![](https://d9hhrg4mnvzow.cloudfront.net/get.classiq.io/office-hour-webinars/36e2928f-logo.svg)](https://www.classiq.io/)
![](https://d9hhrg4mnvzow.cloudfront.net/get.classiq.io/office-hour-webinars/a86d53e3-group-1000014364_10fm0az000000000000028.png)
**_This session:_**  
**From Idea to Quantum Circuit in Minutes with AI Agents**
In this session, we'll take a real-world problem and show you how any team can turn it into a working quantum program in minutes, using natural-language prompts, AI-driven modeling, and automated synthesis. Along the way, we'll walk through the platform, the AI agents, and the visualizer, and show you how easy it is to target any QPU or simulator without rebuilding your circuit. No deep quantum background needed. You'll watch it come together live.
Join **Dr. Erik Garcell, Director of QSA,** and **Dr. John (Jack) Glennon, Quantum Applications Engineer,** as they build a working quantum application live, start to finish, and show you exactly how it's done.  

What You’ll Gain from This Webinar
1
**Turn Real-World Problems into Quantum Circuits**  

See a domain challenge become a running quantum application in minutes, not months.
2
**Let our expert quantum engineering agents do the heavy lifting**  

Watch natural-language prompts and automated synthesis handle the modeling and circuit engineering for you.
3
**Build at a higher level**  

See how Classiq's abstractions remove low-level circuit work, so you focus on the problem, not the gates.
4
**Optimize and run on any hardware**  

Synthesize, optimize, and deploy the same application across real quantum hardware and simulators.
5
**Start building today**  

Learn how your team can begin creating scalable quantum applications without deep quantum expertise.
**Dr. Erik Garcell**
Director of Quantum Solutions Architecture at Classiq Technologies. Erik works with enterprise and research organizations to identify high-impact quantum computing applications, develop adoption roadmaps, and deploy scalable quantum workflows.  
Holding a Ph.D. in Physics and an M.S. in Technical Entrepreneurship and Management from the University of Rochester, Erik operates at the intersection of advanced technology, business strategy, and scientific communication. At the core of his work is the translation of complex quantum concepts into practical business and research initiatives, helping organizations move from quantum curiosity to real-world implementation.
Director of QSA, Classiq

Quantum Applications Engineer at Classiq Technologies, helping enterprise organizations design and implement quantum applications for scientific and industrial use cases. He holds a Ph.D. in Materials Science and Engineering from Boston University, where his research focused on computational modeling and materials simulation. Drawing on experience in management consulting and scientific software development, Jack translates domain-specific challenges into quantum algorithmic approaches that are accessible, practical, and extensible. His work enables teams to turn quantum concepts into working solutions for real-world problems.
